titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 20 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Bliss Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender (1876-1960) to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). This letter is actually comprised of three separate sheets of paper, sent on the same date, but described by Spender as 'scrawls' written in his spare time. Spender discusses financial matters, plans for his upcoming leave, and Lillian's current moves to secure a new house. He expresses frustration that he has received little news of his brother Rex. In the second section of the letter, Spender suggests that as 'Mab's children may be having a dull time, the Spenders' 'Xmas gift' to them could be 'an outing in town'. He also notes that he has enclosed a cheque for three pounds. In the third section, Spender recounts a recent trip to the trenches, and mentions his gladness that Lillian has 'hit it off so well with Lady C'.Sir Wilfrid Spender was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender wrote a collection of letters to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Posted 20th I have a little spare time tonight whilst the others are out
so will begin a scrawl tho' it probably won't be worth posting
till Tuesday. 1 st business. You might send for my passbook to be ready
for me if I turn up, but don't ask for it till the 27th. I shall
also want 'my' cash. box please. I shall probably turn
up travelling quite light with only my rucksack with underclothes & trousers so
shall be glad if you can turn out a pair of boots, shoes, trouser & a
bag if I'm to travel anywhere. I think those 5% bonds may be the things for you as
you will get good interest & should have no depreciation 5 years
hence. I wish I could say the same of the past ! I have cashed a further for Â£fco 125. I am longing to hear about the house & whether you have
taken it. I expect now you will be simply dismayed at not having
got a burrow in time for b w. Shall he postpone his leave
if he gets it till Feb 7 ( & probably a good bit further too ). I cannot understand about Rex. Has he already gone to
Havre with a draft or as conducting officer ? The family are
very bad at forwarding letters & I almost think they had better
come via me in spite of the time.
All my Xmas letters will be reserved strictly till the 25th. Very
good of me as they give me
quite a lot of curiosity already, as I only half remember them. The date provisionally fixed for me is 1st Jan 17 Victoria 7.45 pm
if I turn up it will be a deferred Xmas gift. Poor pr nothing but
an empty pawed BW. I hear 107th are not in the lines but shall
hope to see something of them. Your bw Genl back from boar hunt. I think he caught
the spirit of the chase, for his temper is boorish ! He ought to
sleep well. 20 Dec
As Mab's children
seem likely to have a
dull time perhaps we
could give them an outing
in town as our Xmas
gift. Any how I leave
this to you & Mab I have written a cheque
for Â£300. to Jimpart
for the other maids at 24
Dec 20 Just back from a very pleasant day in the
trenches with 107 Bsc. The have done
quite well. The men are anxious to come
back to the Div. The Officers are not!
Douglas was with me. No more now. Your B W WBS ( 2 letters from you today but rather short.
So glad you hit it off so well with Lady C & Lady F.
Very likely they will appear to cool off a bit later when
less keen on the work 1ststill possible unless Huns attack.
Dec 20
